title: Acmena dispansa
intro: Acmena dissandra, commonly known as the lilly pilly, is a species of plant native to Australia, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. The lilly pilly has been traditionally used in Australian folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and sore throats, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Acmena dissandra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, the lilly pilly is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
